# Galaxy Empire Clone — Final Elephant Carpaccio Backlog

**Stack:** Svelte → Nginx → Go Gateway → Microservices → PostgreSQL → Redis
**Resources:** Metal, Crystal, Gas (mined) + Energy (generated by Solar Plant/Fusion Reactor/Solar Satellites)

---

## Architecture

### Network Topology — 3-Tier Isolation

```
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║             PUBLIC NETWORK                       ║
║  ┌──────────┐                                    ║
║  │  User    │── HTTPS :443                       ║
║  │  Browser │                                    ║
║  └────┬─────┘                                    ║
╚═══════╪══════════════════════════════════════════╝
        │
╔════════╪══════════════════════════════════════════╗
║        ▼           INTERNAL NETWORK               ║
║  ┌─────────────┐                                  ║
║  │    Nginx    │ :80 (Svelte static + /api/*)     ║
║  │ (reverse    │                                  ║
║  │  proxy)     │── proxy_pass → gateway:8080      ║
║  └──────┬──────┘                                  ║
║         │                                         ║
║  ┌──────▼──────┐                                  ║
║  │   Gateway   │ JWT validate, rate limit, route  ║
║  │  (chi:8080) │─────────────────────┐             ║
║  └──────┬──────┘                     │             ║
║         │                            │             ║
║     ┌───┼──────┬──────┬──────┬──────┼──┐          ║
║     ▼   ▼      ▼      ▼      ▼      ▼  ▼          ║
║  ┌────┐ ┌────┐ ┌────┐ ┌────┐ ┌────┐ ┌────┐ ┌────┐║
║  │Auth│ │Plan│ │Fleet│ │Com.│ │Gal.│ │Res.│ │Al. │║
║  │8081│ │et  │ │8083 │ │8084│ │8085│ │8086│ │8087│║
║  └────┘ │8082│ └────┘ └────┘ └────┘ └────┘ └────┘║
║         └────┘  ┌────┐ ┌────┐                     ║
║                 │Neb.│ │Not.│                     ║
║                 │8088│ │8089│                     ║
║                 └────┘ └────┘                     ║
╚══════════════════╪══════════╪═════════════════════╝
                   │          │
╔══════════════════╪══════════╪═════════════════════╗
║                  ▼          ▼    PRIVATE NETWORK   ║
║  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐             ║
║  │  PostgreSQL 16  │  │   Redis 7    │             ║
║  │  :5432          │  │  :6379       │             ║
║  │  schemas:       │  │  Streams     │             ║
║  │  auth, planet,  │  │  Cache       │             ║
║  │  fleet, combat, │  │  Rate limit  │             ║
║  │  galaxy, ...    │  │  counters    │             ║
║  └─────────────────┘  └──────────────┘             ║
╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

### Network Rules

| Network | Accessible From | Contains | Ports |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Public** | Internet | Nginx | 80/443 |
| **Internal** | Nginx, all services | Gateway + Auth + Planet + Fleet + Combat + Galaxy + Research + Alliance + Nebula + Notification | 8080–8089 |
| **Private** | Services only | PostgreSQL, Redis | 5432, 6379 |

- Services reach each other via **internal DNS**: `http://auth:8081`, `http://planet:8082`, etc.
- PostgreSQL and Redis have **no public endpoint**. Not exposed through Nginx or any LoadBalancer.
- In Docker Compose: separate `networks:` blocks (`public`, `internal`, `private`) with per-service `networks:` attachments.
- In K8s: `NetworkPolicy` resources enforce isolation. PG and Redis are `ClusterIP` only.

### Services (10)

| Service | Go Package | Internal Port | Responsibility |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Gateway** | `cmd/gateway` | 8080 | Auth validation, rate limiting, request routing, single public entry point |
| **Auth** | `cmd/auth` | 8081 | Register, login, JWT issue/refresh, password reset |
| **Planet** | `cmd/planet` | 8082 | Planets, buildings, construction queue, resource generation, energy balance, storage |
| **Fleet** | `cmd/fleet` | 8083 | Ships, fleet movement, fuel calc, fleet management (split/merge/recall), fleet missions |
| **Combat** | `cmd/combat` | 8084 | 6-round combat engine, wipe check, CAR, damage calc, combat reports, debris, plundering |
| **Galaxy** | `cmd/galaxy` | 8085 | Galaxy map (3D coords), system detail, position state |
| **Research** | `cmd/research` | 8086 | Research lab, tech tree (16+3 techs), tech gating, research queue |
| **Alliance** | `cmd/alliance` | 8087 | Alliances, members, roles, bank, bulletin board |
| **Nebula** | `cmd/nebula` | 8088 | Expedition outcomes, DM earnings, enemy scaling, nebula rewards |
| **Notification** | `cmd/notification` | 8089 | Notifications, SSE push, chat (global + alliance), in-game messaging |

### Services (10)

| Service | Go Package | Responsibility |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Gateway** | `cmd/gateway` | Auth validation, rate limiting, request routing, single public entry point |
| **Auth** | `cmd/auth` | Register, login, JWT issue/refresh, password reset |
| **Planet** | `cmd/planet` | Planets, buildings, construction queue, resource generation, energy balance, storage |
| **Fleet** | `cmd/fleet` | Ships, fleet movement, fuel calc, fleet management (split/merge/recall), fleet missions |
| **Combat** | `cmd/combat` | 6-round combat engine, wipe check, CAR, damage calc, combat reports, debris, plundering |
| **Galaxy** | `cmd/galaxy` | Galaxy map (3D coords), system detail, position state |
| **Research** | `cmd/research` | Research lab, tech tree (16+3 techs), tech gating, research queue |
| **Alliance** | `cmd/alliance` | Alliances, members, roles, bank, bulletin board |
| **Nebula** | `cmd/nebula` | Expedition outcomes, DM earnings, enemy scaling, nebula rewards |
| **Notification** | `cmd/notification` | Notifications, SSE push, chat (global + alliance), in-game messaging |

### Tech Choices

| Layer | Choice | Rationale |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Go HTTP** | `go-chi/chi/v5` | Lightweight, idiomatic, stdlib-compatible router |
| **DB driver** | `jackc/pgx/v5` | Best Go PostgreSQL driver, connection pooling via pgxpool |
| **Migrations** | `pressly/goose` | File-based, embeddable, straightforward |
| **Redis** | `redis/go-redis/v9` | De facto standard, Redis Streams for async queues |
| **JWT** | `golang-jwt/jwt/v5` | Standard, well-maintained |
| **Async queues** | Redis Streams | Reuse Redis, consumer groups for workers |
| **Observability** | OTel SDK | Prometheus (metrics) + Tempo (traces) + Loki (logs) |
| **Auth** | bcrypt + JWT HS256 | Simple, well-understood, no PKI overhead for MVP |
| **Real-time** | SSE via Notification service | Simpler than WebSockets for unidirectional server→client |
| **Logging** | `log/slog` (Go 1.21+) | Structured, leveled, stdlib |

### Service-to-Service Communication
- **REST/HTTP** for MVP (not gRPC). Gateway calls services via internal HTTP.
- Services call each other via HTTP when needed (e.g., Combat calls Fleet to read ship stats).
- gRPC can be introduced per-service later if latency becomes a concern.

### Database Strategy
- **Single PostgreSQL instance** with **one schema per service** (`auth`, `planet`, `fleet`, etc.)
- Services only read/write their own schema. Foreign keys reference IDs across schemas.
- This keeps MVP simple while leaving the door open to extract per-service databases into separate RDS instances later.

### Infrastructure

| Environment | Setup |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Local dev** | `docker compose up` — all services + PG + Redis + Nginx + Prometheus + Grafana |
| **Production** | Kubernetes via **Helm charts** (umbrella chart + per-service chart). Managed PG (RDS / CloudNativePG). Redis cluster. Nginx Ingress Controller. HPA on CPU/memory. |
| **Service mesh** | Skipped for MVP (no Istio/Envoy). Direct pod-to-pod. K8s DNS + readiness probes handle routing. |

---

## Production-Grade Standards

Every service must follow these rules:

### Error Handling
- Wrap errors with context: `fmt.Errorf("process order %d: %w", id, err)`
- Define sentinel errors (`var ErrNotFound = errors.New("not found")`) for domain-level failures
- HTTP handlers return structured JSON errors: `{"error": {"code": "NOT_FOUND", "message": "..."}}`
- Panics are never caught — let the service crash and restart via K8s

### Logging
- Use `log/slog` with structured attributes, not formatted strings
- Every request gets a trace ID propagated via context
- Log levels: `DEBUG` (development), `INFO` (significant events), `WARN` (expected failures), `ERROR` (unexpected failures)
- Never log secrets, passwords, or full request bodies

### Health Checks
- Every service exposes `GET /healthz` (liveness) and `GET /readyz` (readiness)
- Readiness checks DB connectivity and upstream dependencies
- K8s probes configured in Helm chart

### Graceful Shutdown
- `signal.Notify(ctx, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)`
- `http.Server.Shutdown()` with configurable timeout (30s default)
- Drain Redis consumer groups before shutting down workers

### Context Propagation
- Every DB query, HTTP call, and async worker receives a `context.Context`
- Timeouts cascade: gateway sets a deadline → propagated to downstream services
- OTel trace context propagated via W3C `traceparent` header

### Security
- Parameterized queries always (never string interpolation in SQL)
- Input validation on every public endpoint (length, format, range checks)
- CORS restricted to known origins in production
- Rate limiting in gateway: per-endpoint, per-IP, per-user token buckets via Redis
- Secrets via environment variables (K8s secrets → env vars)

### Code Organization (per service)

```
cmd/planet/
├── main.go           # wire up dependencies, start HTTP server
├── handler.go        # HTTP handlers
├── service.go        # business logic
├── repository.go     # PostgreSQL queries
├── repository_test.go
├── service_test.go
├── handler_test.go
├── models.go         # domain types
└── middleware.go     # service-specific middleware
```

---

## Testing Strategy

### Approach: Test-Driven Development (TDD)
Every feature follows **Red-Green-Refactor**:
1. Write a failing test
2. Write the minimum code to pass
3. Refactor while keeping tests green

### Test Pyramid

```
        ╱╲
       ╱ E2E ╲           ← 1 per critical user journey
      ╱────────╲
     ╱Integration╲       ← service boundary, DB, Redis
    ╱──────────────╲
   ╱   Unit tests   ╲    ← business logic, pure functions
  ╱────────────────────╲
```

| Layer | Tool | What | Run frequency |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Unit** | `go test` + `testing` stdlib | Pure business logic, no deps. Table-driven tests. | On every save |
| **Integration** | `go test` + `testcontainers-go` | Repository layer with real PG + Redis containers. HTTP handler tests with `httptest`. | On every push |
| **Contract** | Custom | API response shapes between services (Gateway ↔ service). | Before deploy |
| **E2E** | `playwright` (Svelte) + custom Go runner | Full flow: Svelte → Nginx → Gateway → services → PG. Deployed to ephemeral K8s namespace. | Before release |
| **Load** | `k6` | Fleet movement, combat resolution, resource tick under load. Identify bottlenecks. | Weekly |

### Unit Testing Conventions
- Table-driven tests with descriptive names
- Mock interfaces for service dependencies (repository is an interface, service tests mock it)
- No `require` assertions — use `t.Fatalf` or `cmp.Diff` with `t.Errorf`
- Test files live next to the code they test (`service.go` → `service_test.go`)

### Integration Testing
- Use `testcontainers-go` to spin up ephemeral PG + Redis containers per test suite
- Run migrations once per package `TestMain`
- Each test case gets a clean transaction (rolled back after) — never share state between tests

### Fixtures & Test Data
- Ship stats, building configs, tech tree = Go structs in `internal/data/` (not DB-seeded)
- Test helpers in `internal/testutil/` — factories for planets, fleets, etc.

### CI Pipeline
```
push → lint (golangci-lint) → unit → integration → build → docker → (deploy to staging)
```
- Lint + unit must pass in < 2 min
- Integration in < 5 min
- Block merge if any stage fails

---

## Phase 0: Foundation (01–05)

| # | Name | UI / Input | Business Logic | Data / Storage |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **01** | Walking skeleton | Svelte loads, `GET /api/planet/1`, displays name + Metal/Crystal/Gas/Energy | Gateway routes to planet service, returns hardcoded JSON | In-memory, no DB |
| **02** | Auth: register + login | Register form (email/pw), Login form, redirect to dashboard | Auth service bcrypt-hashes, issues JWT, gateway validates | `auth_users` table |
| **03** | Planet dashboard | Dashboard shows real planet name + resource amounts from DB, building list | Planet service reads user's home planet | `planets` table, seeded per user |
| **04** | Resource generation | Resources tick up every 5s via SSE/poll, UI numbers increment. 3 mines (Metal/Crystal/Gas) + Solar Plant (energy) | Rate from building levels. Building time formula: `t = A/(r+1) × e^(B×l)` where A=71.3, B=0.406, r=Robotics level | `resources` + `buildings` (mine, solar plant) |
| **05** | Storage limits | Progress bar shows fill % vs storage cap per resource type. Overflow stops production | Production caps at storage level. Metal/Crystal/Gas storage buildings | `buildings` subtype=storage |

---

## Phase 1: Construction & Economy (06–13)

| # | Name | UI / Input | Business Logic | Data / Storage |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **06** | Energy balance | Green/red bolt icon. Negative → production penalty %. | `energyProd - buildingConsumption = net`. Energy Tech reduces consumption. Solar Satellites (ships) add orbital energy. | Computed per planet from buildings + orbiting Solar Sats |
| **07** | Build queue | Click building → cost shown → confirm → countdown bar → level increments | Validate resource cost, async worker completes on timer | `construction_queue` |
| **08** | Robotics Facility + Nanite Factory | Robotics Facility building reduces building construction time. Nanite Factory reduces all build times (buildings, ships, defenses) | Robotics: `t / (1+RFlevel)`. Nanite: `t / 2^NFlevel` (50% per level 1-3, 25% per level 4-10). Costs all 3 resources | Building types with speed bonus config |
| **09** | Planet fields + Terraformer | Building grid shows slots remaining (e.g. 38/40), can't build if full. Terraformer adds fields permanently | `buildings.count < planet.max_fields` check. Terraformer completion bumps max_fields | `planets.max_fields` |
| **10** | Construction management | Cancel in-progress build → 50% resource refund. Deconstruct existing building → countdown → field freed + partial refund | Queue worker checks cancellation flag. Reverse queue for deconstruction | `construction_queue.status`, building level decremented |
| **11** | Multiple planets + types + temperature | Sidebar lists all user planets, click to switch. Types: terran/desert/ice/volcanic/gas giant with distinct appearance. Temperature displayed | Temperature affects: cold = more Gas production (1-1.5 levels), hot = more solar energy. 25% variance based on position. | `planets.type`, `planets.temperature` |
| **12** | Fusion Reactor | Buildable once Gas Mine Lv5 + Energy Tech Lv3. Produces significant energy, consumes Gas per hour | Energy output formula with diminishing returns. Energy Tech level increases output | `buildings` subtype=fusion_reactor |
| **13** | VIP + Official Rank | VIP: daily login accumulates points, unlocks production bonuses. Official Rank: titles earned through play, affects output | % modifiers applied to resource production formulas | `player.vip_level`, `player.official_rank` |

---

## Phase 2: Galaxy & Fleet (14–24)

| # | Name | UI / Input | Business Logic | Data / Storage |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **14** | Galaxy map (3D coords) | Coordinate format `[Galaxy:System:Position]`. Browse galaxies → systems → view system detail with 15 positions. | Galaxy service generates universe seed. Each galaxy contains many systems, each system has 15 positions. | `galaxy_systems` with coordinate hierarchy |
| **15** | Galaxy detail (15 positions) | Click a system → shows all 15 positions: planets 1-9 (owned/colonizable), debris 10-12 (active/empty), moons 13-15 | Resolves position state from planets, debris_fields, moons tables. Positions 4-6 = most fields for colonization. | `planet_coordinates`, `debris_fields`, `moons` |
| **16** | Fleet dispatch + mission types | Dedicated page: select ships from planet, set target coordinates, choose mission type (Attack / ACS Attack / ACS Defend / Transport / Deploy / Espionage / Colonize / Expedition / Recycle), set speed | UI composition — reads fleet + galaxy data. Mission type determines logic on arrival. | Fleet mission types enum |
| **17** | Shipyard + all ships | Shipyard building → list all buildable ships. Civil: Cargo, Large Cargo, Recycler, Probe, Colony Ship, Solar Satellite. Combat: Light Fighter, Heavy Fighter, Cruiser, Battleship, Dreadnought, Bomber, Destroyer | Each ship type has: Strength, Shield, Attack, Speed, Cargo Cap, Fuel Usage, resource costs. Building/tech requirements per ship. | Fleet table, ship model configs |
| **18** | Batch building | "Build 1 / 10 / 100 / Max" buttons alongside single build | Formula: `T(h) = ((M+C)/(11132×(ShipyardLevel+1))) / 2^NaniteLevel`. Batch validates total cost, multi-row insert. | Multi-row insert in fleet, resource cost batch validation |
| **19** | Fleet movement + speed slider + fuel | Speed slider (10%-100%) affects ETA and gas cost. Fleet in transit with ETA countdown. Fuel deducted from planet on departure. | Travel time = `distance / minShipSpeed × (100/speed%)`. Gas = `∑(ships × fuelUsage) × distanceFactor × speedFactor`. Bombers can't fly alone (fuel 1000 > cargo 500). | `fleet.status`, `travel_queue`, `fleet.speed_pct`, fuel deducted from planet resources |
| **20** | Drive techs + ship speed | Combustion Drive, Impulse Drive, Hyperspace Drive each boost specific ship speeds by 30% per level | `shipSpeed × (1 + driveLevel × 0.3)` applied to movement calculation. Hyperspace Drive affects BS/Dread/Destroyer/Interceptor/Iron Behemoth. | `technologies` levels applied per ship type |
| **21** | Fleet management | Fleet slots capped by Computer Tech. Split (select subset → new fleet), Merge (combine fleets at same location), Recall (reverse travel, partial fuel cost) | `fleetCount <= 1 + computerTechLevel × 2`. Admiral commander adds +2-3 slots. | Fleet row management, status changes |
| **22** | Transport + Deploy | Transport: send resources to any player planet (deliver + return). Deploy: permanently station fleet at own planet (fleet transfers, no return). | Transport delivers resources on arrival, fleet returns. Deploy changes fleet ownership to destination planet. | Resource delta between planets, fleet owner/planet change |
| **23** | Fleet viz on galaxy | Fleet icons with direction arrows shown on galaxy map. Active travel routes visible. | Galaxy service returns active fleet positions for current player | Read-only query from fleet service |
| **24** | Attack cooldown | 2h cooldown between attacks on same target coordinates. UI shows remaining cooldown. | Attack service checks `last_attack_time` per target, rejects if <2h | `attack_cooldowns` table |

## Phase 3: Combat (25–36)

| # | Name | UI / Input | Business Logic | Data / Storage |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **25** | Espionage | Click player → "Spy" → send Espionage Probes → timer → report: resources, fleet, defense, tech levels | Success and detail level based on relative Espionage Tech. **Also increases nebula resource rewards.** Higher tech = more detail. | `espionage_reports` |
| **26** | Combat engine (6 rounds) | Fleet arrives at occupied planet → auto-resolve → combat report. 6 rounds max. | **Defender fires FIRST each round.** Death order: LF → HF → Cruiser → BS → Dread → Bomber → Destroyer → Interceptor → Iron Behemoth. Shields reset each round. | `combat_reports` with round-by-round data |
| **27** | Wipe check + damage absorption | At start of each round: if one side's attack ≥ ~1.5× enemy defense, weaker side wiped. Otherwise ~50% of incoming damage absorbed. | Remaining damage applied down death order. Ships destroyed when hull reaches 0. | Part of combat engine |
| **28** | Consecutive Attack Rate | Each ship type has CAR targets. Single proc per round per ship type. | `rf_chance = 1 - 1/rfAmount`. Roll per ship type each round. Dreadnought: vs HF(4), Cruiser(4), BS(7). Others TBD. | `ship_consecutive_attack` config table |
| **29** | Bomber damage vs defenses | Bombers deal bonus damage to defense structures. Light Lasers take 2× damage from Bombers. | Damage modifier applied when attacker is Bomber vs defense targets | Combat modifier config |
| **30** | Plundering | Winner steals `min(loserResources × 0.5, totalCargoCapacity)` per resource type | Only surviving cargo ships contribute to cargo capacity. Resources transferred. | Resource delta in combat report |
| **31** | Combat reports | Detailed report: round-by-round losses, ships before/after per side, resources stolen, debris created, attackers/defenders | Combat service returns structured JSON, stored for 7 days | `combat_reports` with embedded round data |
| **32** | Defense structures | Rocket Launcher, Light Laser, Heavy Laser, MK2 Cannon, Ion Cannon, Plasma Cannon, Proton Cannon. Build in Shipyard defense tab. | Defense stats (Strength/Shield/Attack) factored into combat. Consume planet fields. | `defenses` table per planet |
| **33** | Shield Dome | Small Shield Dome + Large Shield Dome. Max 1 of each per planet. "Only after destroyed, can you construct another." | Shield Dome absorbs damage before other defenses. Large = 10k/10k/1. | `defenses` subtype=shield, unique per planet |
| **34** | Defense repair + Engineer | ~70% of surviving defenses repaired after combat. Engineer commander (100 DM/30d) reduces losses: Lv1=retain 50%, Lv2=70%, Lv3=75%. +Fusion output. | After combat, roll repair per defense unit. Engineer buff modifies destruction rate. | Player's active Engineer buff from DM purchase |
| **35** | ACS Attack + Defend | Alliance members coordinate: multiple fleets arrive at same target simultaneously. All attacker fleets combined. All defender fleets combined. | Combat service aggregates all fleet groups by side. Loot distributed proportionally by cargo capacity. | `fleet.alliance_group_id`, `fleet.mission=acs_attack/defend` |
| **36** | Debris + Recyclers | Combat creates debris field at coordinates: 30% of destroyed ships' (metal + crystal) cost. Send Recyclers with mission=Recycle → harvest. | Debris field has total metal + crystal. Recyclers have 20k cargo each. Field depleted as harvested. Moon creation: 400k debris = +1%, max 20% at 8M. | `debris_fields` with metal/crystal amounts |

## Phase 4: Expansion (37–42)

| # | Name | UI / Input | Business Logic | Data / Storage |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **37** | Colony ships + colonize | Colony Ship → empty position → new planet created. Random size 60-280 fields. Requires Astrophysics ≥ 2. | Fleet triggers planet creation on arrival, Colony Ship consumed. Max planets = `1 + Astrophysics/2` (up to ~9+). | `planets` insert, fleet consumed |
| **38** | Planet temperature effects | Temperature displayed per planet. Affects: cold = more Gas (+1-1.5 effective levels), hot = more solar energy | Production formulas include temperature coefficient. Set at creation based on position in system. | `planets.temperature` set at creation |
| **39** | Moon creation | Debris field ≥200k → 20% chance moon forms at debris position. Each +400k debris = +1%, max 20% at 8M. | Combat service rolls moon creation after debris generation. Random moon size 20-50 fields average. | `moons` table, linked to planet coordinates |
| **40** | Moon buildings | Moon Base (+3 slots/level), Robotics Facility, Shipyard, Pioneer Lab, EU-X Radar Array, Wormhole Generator | Moon is secondary base with its own building set and field limits. Moon Base required first. | `moons.buildings` |
| **41** | Wormhole Generator | Instant fleet transport between 2 moons with generators. No gas cost. No resources. 1h cooldown on sending moon. | Requires Moon Base Lv3 + Robotics Lv3. Build cost: 1.6M/3.2M/1.6M. Fleet teleports instantly, cooldown tracked. | `wormhole_generators` with linked pair + cooldown |
| **42** | Pioneer Lab + Iron Behemoth | Build Iron Behemoth (super-ship: 350k/4k/5.5k stats) at Pioneer Lab, not Shipyard. Research moon techs here. | Iron Behemoth costs Alloy Fragments + resources. Pioneer Lab also researches Alloy Detection Tech, Dynamic Power Tech, Combined Guidance Tech. | `pioneer_lab` building, Iron Behemoth in fleet |

## Phase 5: Nebula & Dark Matter (43–49)

| # | Name | UI / Input | Business Logic | Data / Storage |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **43** | Nebula exploration | "Nebula" tab → select fleet → send. Travel ~5 min, explore ~30 min, return ~5 min (total ~40 min at 100%). Max simultaneous = Astro level. 16 runs/day base. | Fleet mission=expedition. Speed slider affects only travel portion (~5 min each way). Explore duration is fixed. | `fleet.mission=expedition`, timer management |
| **44** | Nebula outcomes | On return: resources (up to 2M each at 667 recyclers), ships (LF/HF/etc), Dark Matter, pirates (-30%), aliens (+30%), or nothing | Weighted outcome table. 667 recyclers = max resource return. Espionage Tech increases reward probability. | `expedition_outcomes`, RNG-based |
| **45** | Enemy scaling in nebula | Pirates = 30% weaker than fleet's strongest ship. Aliens = 30% stronger. Recyclers optimal (attack=1 → weakest enemies). | Enemy fleet generated based on fleet composition. Recyclers never take part in combat (attack=1 means enemies are 0.7/1.3). | Procedurally generated enemy fleet |
| **46** | Dark Matter earnings | DM from nebula (max 50 per fleet, ~1/9-10 chance), 26 intro quests (~40 DM total), invite system, daily tasks | DM tracked per player. Nebula: >100 ships in fleet = max DM chance per roll. | `player.dark_matter` balance, DM transaction log |
| **47** | DM spending | Speed up: 15 min = 1 DM, 30 min = 2 DM (tiered at each 15 min increment). Hire commanders. Buy Galactonite shards. | DM deducted on spend. "Speed Up" flow: button → confirm → DM cost shown. Can speed research/ship/build. | `player.dark_matter`, timer modification |
| **48** | Commander system | 6 commanders × 3 levels. Lv1 = DM (25-100 for 7-30d). Lv2-3 = Credits. | **Commander**: -build time, +queues. **Engineer**: +fusion output, defense retain. **Technocrat**: +espionage, -research time. **Admiral**: +fleet slots, -defense cost. **Guider**: retain expedition fleet. **Geologist**: +mine output. | `player_commanders` with type, level, expiry |
| **49** | Credits system | 2nd premium currency. Earned through gameplay. Used for Lv2-3 commanders, Space Store items. | Credits balance tracked separately from DM. Earned via achievements/events. | `player.credits` balance |

## Phase 6: Research (50–54)

| # | Name | UI / Input | Business Logic | Data / Storage |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **50** | Research Lab + queue | Build Research Lab → select tech → queue → countdown → tech level unlocks. Shared across all planets. Speed = highest Lab level. | 16 planet techs + 3 moon techs. Each has prerequisites. Cost formula escalates per level. Technocrat commander reduces research time. | `research_queue`, `technologies` per player |
| **51** | Basic Tech (5) | Energy Tech, Laser Tech, Ion Tech, Hyperspace Tech, Plasma Tech | Energy Tech: reduces building consumption, increases Fusion output. Laser/Ion/Hyperspace/Plasma: unlock ships + defenses. Weapon/Shield/Strength = +10%/level to all ships. | `technologies` with category, per-player level |
| **52** | Advanced Tech (5) | Astrophysics (planets + nebula fleets), Computer (fleet slots), Espionage (spy success + nebula rewards), Ultra Temperature, Anti-Gravity | Each level provides a specific progression benefit. Astrophysics Lv4=2 neb fleets, Lv9=3, Lv12=4. | `technologies` with associated effects |
| **53** | Combat Tech (6) | Combustion Drive, Impulse Drive, Hyperspace Drive, Weapons Tech, Shielding Tech, Strength Tech | Each drive type boosts specific ship speeds by 30%/level. Weapons/Shielding/Strength boost corresponding ship stats by 10%/level for ALL ships. | `technologies` with stat bonus formulas |
| **54** | Moon Tech (3) | Alloy Detection Tech, Dynamic Power Tech, Combined Guidance Tech. Researched in Pioneer Lab on moon. | Effects undocumented — use OGame-equivalent behavior: Alloy = debris yield, Dynamic Power = moon energy, Combined Guidance = fleet. | `moon_technologies` table |

---

## Phase 7: Radar & Detection (55–57)

| # | Name | UI / Input | Business Logic | Data / Storage |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **55** | Radar Facility | Planet building. Detects incoming attacks and espionage. Level = detection range/accuracy. | Unsafe state shown on planet list: "Secure" → "Espionaged" → "Attack is running" with red count number. Attack origin coordinates revealed. | `buildings` subtype=radar, `radar_events` |
| **56** | EU-X Radar | Moon building. "Scan" function: see fleets heading to selected planet. Undetected by target. | Range depends on EU-X level. Cannot see fleets LEAVING the target. Undetectable scan. | `moons.buildings` subtype=eu_x_radar, scan range formula |
| **57** | Radar detection mechanics | Radar detects probe landings and incoming attacks. Notification generated. Galactonite bonuses on planet do NOT affect fleets launched from moon. | If planet under protection, can still launch attack from moon without removing protection. | Detection logic in fleet movement service |

---

## Phase 8: Advanced Systems (58–62)

| # | Name | UI / Input | Business Logic | Data / Storage |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **58** | Star Gate | Planet building. Paired gates: build on 2 planets → instant fleet travel between them. | Fleet moves instantly between linked Star Gates. Build + upgrade costs all 3 resources. | `star_gates` with linked pairs |
| **59** | Galactonite Research Center | Planet building. 3 gem slots per planet. Equip gems for % stat bonuses. | Flaming Crystal (+attack%), Concentrated Galactonite (+armor%), Galactonite Shield (+strength%). Gem star level 1-5 determines bonus magnitude. | `galactonite_gems` per planet slot |
| **60** | Galactonite shard system | Galactonite Discoverer (upgraded with DM) determines shard acquisition rate. Shards from merchant boxes + attacking NPCs. Combine shards → gems. | After 20 attempts, probability changes. Higher Discoverer level = more shards per box + higher drop rate. | `galactonite_shards`, `galactonite_discoverer` level |
| **61** | Missile system | Missile Silo building → build IPMs (Interplanetary Missiles) + ABMs (Anti-Ballistic Missiles). IPMs target enemy defenses. ABMs intercept incoming IPMs. | Missile combat resolved before ship combat phase. IPMs have flight time based on distance. ABMs auto-intercept based on count. | `missiles` table, separate missile combat phase |
| **62** | Alliance Depot | Planet building. Allows alliance members basic interaction. Upgrades improve functionality. | Only non-upgradeable building. Basic alliance logistics. | `buildings` subtype=alliance_depot |

---

## Phase 9: Social (63–68)

| # | Name | UI / Input | Business Logic | Data / Storage |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **63** | Alliance: create/join | Alliance tab → name + tag → Create. Search/apply → Join. | Alliance service manages groups. Founder assigns Officer/Member roles. | `alliances`, `alliance_members` |
| **64** | Alliance bank + roles | Donate resources to shared pool. Withdraw based on role permissions. Audit log. | Founder: full control. Officer: manage invites, bulletin. Member: donate, view bank. | `alliance_resources`, `alliance_members.role` |
| **65** | Alliance features | Bulletin board (officer posts announcements). Member list with online status. Share battle reports to alliance. | CRUD with role-based write permissions. Shared reports visible to all members. | `alliance_bulletins`, shared report flags |
| **66** | Global + Alliance chat | Real-time chat: Global channel (all players), Alliance channel (members only). Multi-language support. | Chat service manages channels, message history, rate limiting. SSE push for real-time. | `chat_messages` with channel type |
| **67** | In-game messaging | Compose, inbox, outbox, delete between players. System messages for attacks/espionage detected/events. | Simple messaging, read/unread state, system-generated events with links to reports. | `messages` table |
| **68** | Buddy list | Add/remove friends, view online/offline status | Friend service manages bidirectional relationships. | `friendships` table |

---

## Phase 10: Meta (69–76)

| # | Name | UI / Input | Business Logic | Data / Storage |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **69** | Rankings (power score) | `/rankings` with tabs: Points / Fleet / Research / Defense. Player search. | Power score = `(metal + 2×crystal + 3×gas) / 1000`. Ships, buildings, research all counted. Refreshed every 10 min. <1000 score: fight anyone. >1000: only attack within 10× range. | `player_scores` materialized view |
| **70** | Achievements + quests | 26 intro quests (first mine, first ship, first attack). Daily Gift (login reward). Daily Tasks (repeatable). Events. | Quest state machine per player, checks conditions. Rewards: DM, resources, ships. | `quest_progress`, `quest_definitions`, `daily_tasks` |
| **71** | Vacation mode | Toggle → 48h activation → invulnerable, production stops, fleets auto-recall. Block all attacks/messages. | Vacation service schedules shield expiration. Cannot attack while in vacation. | `player.mode = vacation`, `vacation_expiry` |
| **72** | Notifications | Bell icon with red badge. Dropdown: build complete, fleet arrived, attacked, espionage detected, message received, event started | Event bus publishes to notification service, SSE push to client | `notifications` table + SSE stream |
| **73** | UI polish | Animated resource bars, ship/building stat tooltips, loading skeletons, error toasts, galaxy animations, planet list status indicators | Pure frontend | N/A |
| **74** | Planet rename | Click planet name → inline edit → save | Simple update endpoint | `planets.name` |
| **75** | Daily Gift + Daily Tasks | Login calendar reward. Repeatable tasks for DM/resources/ships. Streak bonuses. | Track consecutive login days. Task pool rotates daily. | `daily_gift_progress`, `daily_task_completions` |
| **76** | Events | Time-limited events: double resources, special NPCs, faction battles (Fight G.E.F.). Seasonal content. | Event service manages active events, applies modifiers, rewards on completion. | `events`, `event_participation` |

---

## Phase 11: Production Readiness (77–82)

| # | Name | Description |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **77** | DB migrations | `golang-migrate` / `goose`, one command per service database. CI runs migrations on deploy. |
| **78** | Graceful shutdown | SIGTERM → drain HTTP requests, complete in-flight ticks, save state. signal.Notify, http.Shutdown, worker drain pattern. |
| **79** | K8s probes | `/healthz` (liveness) + `/readyz` (readiness) on every microservice. Health check DB pool, upstream deps. |
| **80** | OpenTelemetry | Traces across service boundaries, RED metrics, structured JSON logs. OTel SDK per service, W3C traceparent propagation. |
| **81** | API rate limiting | Per-endpoint, per-user, burst window (e.g. 100 req/min, burst 20). Token bucket middleware in gateway. Redis counters. |
| **82** | CI/CD | GitHub Actions: lint → test → build → docker → deploy to staging K8s. Automated with manual production gate. |

---

## Phase 12: Operations (83–86)

| # | Name | Description |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **83** | Admin panel | User search, planet view, resource edit, DM/Credits grant, ban/unban, message as GM, event management. |
| **84** | Tutorial | Guided first clicks: build mine → build solar → build shipyard → build cargo → send nebula run → attack NPC. Step state machine, DM rewards. |
| **85** | NPC bases + G.E.F. | NPC-owned planets seeded in galaxy. Attack for resources, no retaliation. G.E.F. faction events. Respawn timer. |
| **86** | Space Store | DM + Credits store for items, boosts, speed-ups. Galactonite shards, commander hires, planet shifters. |

---

**Total: 86 vertical slices** — Mirrors every known mechanic from Galaxy Empire (tap4fun, 2012)
